problems.title: Squaring Numbers
problems.description: Square each element of the list [1, 2, 3]. Expected output: [1, 4, 9]. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.prompt: Square each element of the list [1, 2, 3]. Expected output: [1, 4, 9]
problems.starter_code:
from functools import reduce

def main(input_1):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3]
    main(input_1)
problems.solution:
from functools import reduce

def main(input_1):
    squared = list(map(lambda x: x ** 2, input_1))
    print(squared)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3]
    main(input_1)

problems.title: Adding Numbers
problems.description: Add 3 and 4 using a lambda function. Expected output: 7. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Add 3 and 4 using a lambda function. Expected output: 7
problems.starter_code:
from functools import reduce

def main(x, y):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    x = 3

    y = 4
    main(x, y)
problems.solution:
from functools import reduce

def main(x, y):
    add = lambda x, y: x + y
    result = add(x, y)
    print(result)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    x = 3

    y = 4
    main(x, y)

problems.title: Sorting Tuples
problems.description: Sort the list of tuples [(2, 'b'), (1, 'a')] by the second element. Expected output: [(1, 'a'), (2, 'b')]. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.prompt: Sort the list of tuples [(2, 'b'), (1, 'a')] by the second element. Expected output: [(1, 'a'), (2, 'b')]
problems.starter_code:
from functools import reduce

def main(input_1):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [(2, 'b'), (1, 'a')]
    main(input_1)
problems.solution:
from functools import reduce

def main(input_1):
    sorted_tuples = sorted(input_1, key=lambda x: x[1])
    print(sorted_tuples)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [(2, 'b'), (1, 'a')]
    main(input_1)

problems.title: Filtering Even Numbers
problems.description: Filter out the even numbers from the list [3, 2, 6, 8, 4, 6, 2, 9]. Expected output: [2, 6, 8, 4, 6, 2]. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.prompt: Filter out the even numbers from the list [3, 2, 6, 8, 4, 6, 2, 9]. Expected output: [2, 6, 8, 4, 6, 2]
problems.starter_code:
from functools import reduce

def main(nums):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    nums = [3, 2, 6, 8, 4, 6, 2, 9]
    main(nums)
problems.solution:
from functools import reduce

def main(nums):
    evens = list(filter(lambda n: n % 2 == 0, nums))
    print(evens)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    nums = [3, 2, 6, 8, 4, 6, 2, 9]
    main(nums)
notes: Automatic test generation was skipped: unhashable type: 'list'

problems.title: Summation with Reduce
problems.description: Use the reduce function to compute the sum of the list [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. Expected output: 15. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.prompt: Use the reduce function to compute the sum of the list [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. Expected output: 15
problems.starter_code:
from functools import reduce

def main(nums):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    from functools import reduce

    nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    main(nums)
problems.solution:
from functools import reduce

def main(nums):
    sum_of_nums = reduce(lambda a, b: a + b, nums)
    print(sum_of_nums)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    from functools import reduce

    nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    main(nums)

problems.title: Maximum with Reduce
problems.description: Use the reduce function to find the maximum number in the list [47, 11, 42, 102, 13]. Expected output: 102. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.prompt: Use the reduce function to find the maximum number in the list [47, 11, 42, 102, 13]. Expected output: 102
problems.starter_code:
from functools import reduce

def main(nums):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    from functools import reduce

    nums = [47, 11, 42, 102, 13]
    main(nums)
problems.solution:
from functools import reduce

def main(nums):
    max_num = reduce(lambda a, b: a if a > b else b, nums)
    print(max_num)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    from functools import reduce

    nums = [47, 11, 42, 102, 13]
    main(nums)
